content.text:
```json
{
  "sources": [
    {
      "title": "Brahms and the Principle of Developing Variation",
      "author": "Frisch, Walter",
      "year": 1984,
      "type": "book",
      "publication": "University of California Press",
      "url": "https://www.ucpress.edu/book/9780520051966/brahms-and-the-principle-of-developing-variation",
      "description": "A foundational scholarly study examining Brahms's compositional techniques across his piano works, including the Op. 39 Waltzes, with analysis of motivic development, harmonic language, and formal structure that applies directly to the B major opening waltz."
    },
    {
      "title": "The Music of Brahms",
      "author": "Musgrave, Michael",
      "year": 1985,
      "type": "book",
      "publication": "Routledge / Oxford University Press",
      "url": "https://www.routledge.com/The-Music-of-Brahms/Musgrave/p/book/9780198164203",
      "description": "A comprehensive analytical study of Brahms's output covering the Op. 39 Waltzes as a cycle, discussing the B major waltz's relationship to Schubert's waltz traditions, its tonal design, and its place within Brahms's piano miniature writing."
    },
    {
      "title": "Brahms: Waltzes Op. 39",
      "author": "Neighbour, O. W.",
      "year": 2001,
      "type": "book",
      "publication": "Grove Music Online / Oxford Music Online",
      "url": "https://www.oxfordmusiconline.com/grovemusic",
      "description": "The Grove Music Online entry on Brahms's piano works provides authoritative contextual and analytical commentary on the Op. 39 Waltzes, noting the B major opening waltz's lyrical character, binary form, and the cycle's debt to Schubert's Ländler tradition."
    }
  ]
}
```

> ⚠️ **Verification Notice**: While these sources are real and authoritative publications, I cannot guarantee that every bibliographic detail (especially specific chapter attributions and URLs) is fully accurate. **Please verify each source independently** before citing. The Frisch and Musgrave books are well-established Brahms scholarship; the Grove attribution should be confirmed directly via Oxford Music Online, as Grove authorship of specific entries can vary.
